workflow_name: Customer Renewal Email -> Forecast Impact + Path
user_message: Give me the manager view on this Tailspin Toys email thread. Manager is asking for an update today.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-08 08:37 EST
Subject: Re: security posture Discussion - Next Steps

There's been a change in our timeline for security posture. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
Date: 2024-02-12 12:29 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Tailspin Toys security posture

There's been a change in our timeline for security posture. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-07 10:16 EST
Subject: Re: security posture Discussion - Next Steps

Our team encountered some challenges with security posture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Tailspin Toys: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades

required_artifacts: path forward with MACC/funding options if applicable

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF35-B trap (No freshness label on contract data); produces complete Contract state summary with freshness, forecast impact with confidence, response draft (no commercia
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF35-B failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF35-B failure mode (No freshness label on contract data)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF35-B -- No freshness label on contract data
